claim: Nanoparticles can be targeted against specific cell types by incorporating aptamers into lipid nanoparticles.

result.claim: Nanoparticles can be targeted against specific cell types by incorporating aptamers into lipid nanoparticles.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 1469751, "Aptamer-functionalized lipid nanoparticles targeting osteoblasts as a novel RNA interference–based bone anabolic strategy." The abstract of this paper explicitly states the development of "CH6 aptamer–functionalized lipid nanoparticles (LNPs)" and demonstrates their ability to facilitate "in vitro osteoblast-selective uptake" and "in vivo osteoblast-specific Plekho1 gene silencing." This directly confirms that incorporating aptamers into lipid nanoparticles enables targeting against specific cell types (osteoblasts in this instance).
